failedAssertions.reason: Both predicates are visible in the visual builder, but variables are not shared between predicates — auto-generated names are always unique (PeakReported uses v,v1,v2,v3 and TermInStudyTFIDF uses v4,v5,v6). No visual join indication exists.

Suggestions panel shows chips in the initial empty-query state, but after adding predicates it consistently shows 'No suggestions available.' The suggestions are not context-aware while building a query.

Clicking a suggestion chip adds the predicate WITHOUT variable names (e.g., PeakReported() with empty parens). This produces broken Datalog. Clicking from the predicate browser correctly adds variables — the bug is specific to the suggestion-chip click handler.

The variable binding highlighting CSS logic exists but there is no user-facing UI mechanism to create shared variable bindings between predicates. Auto-generated variable names are always unique, so a real user cannot trigger the visualization.

appliedUpdates.description: Noted that the Vite dev server on port 3100 only listens on IPv6 (::1); agent-browser should use http://[::1]:3100 or test with IPv4 explicitly
